payload.query: Studies of aggregate voting typically find that women win at the same or higher rate than do men. Experimental and survey research, however, often finds evidence of negative stereotypes about female candidates. Given this literature, what explains the weak record of female representation in advanced democracies?

articles.title: Read the Supreme Court Decision
articles.abstract: The Supreme Court ruled that the race-conscious admissions programs at Harvard and the University of North Carolina were unlawful, curtailing affirmative action at colleges and universities around the nation.
articles.snippet: The Supreme Court ruled that the race-conscious admissions programs at Harvard and the University of North Carolina were unlawful, curtailing affirmative action at colleges and universities around the nation.

articles.title: There are plenty of polls in Texas, but little consensus.
articles.abstract: Competitive races in both Senate primaries have led to numerous surveys, but primaries can be remarkably difficult to measure.
articles.snippet: Competitive races in both Senate primaries have led to numerous surveys, but primaries can be remarkably difficult to measure.
articles.byline: By Caroline Soler and Christine Zhang

articles.title: Trump’s Push for Election Power Raises Fears He Will ‘Subvert’ Midterms
articles.abstract: The president appears to be undermining Americans’ faith in the outcome, at a moment when Republicans face an uphill climb to keep control of Congress.
articles.snippet: The president appears to be undermining Americans’ faith in the outcome, at a moment when Republicans face an uphill climb to keep control of Congress.
articles.byline: By Shane Goldmacher and Nick Corasaniti

articles.title: Sandra Day O’Connor, First Woman on the Supreme Court, Is Dead at 93
articles.abstract: During a crucial period in American law — when abortion, affirmative action, sex discrimination and voting rights were on the docket — she was the most powerful woman in the country.
articles.snippet: During a crucial period in American law — when abortion, affirmative action, sex discrimination and voting rights were on the docket — she was the most powerful woman in the country.

articles.title: ‘What Has Been Won Can Be Lost’: A Look at the Past and Future of Feminism
articles.abstract: Feminist scholars and activists, including Gloria Steinem and Leymah Gbowee, reflected on a movement that they said remains unfinished.
articles.snippet: Feminist scholars and activists, including Gloria Steinem and Leymah Gbowee, reflected on a movement that they said remains unfinished.

articles.title: What You Taught Me About Texas
articles.abstract: Our readers in the state offer a window into the current center of the political universe.
articles.snippet: Our readers in the state offer a window into the current center of the political universe.

articles.title: Crockett vs. Talarico. Progressive vs. Moderate. Right?
articles.abstract: The Texas Democratic Senate primary is more about persona than policy, and the same is true for many other races.
articles.snippet: The Texas Democratic Senate primary is more about persona than policy, and the same is true for many other races.

articles.title: Kamala Harris Endorses Jasmine Crockett in Texas Senate Race
articles.abstract: The endorsement from the former vice president, her most significant since leaving office, comes as Ms. Crockett is facing a competitive Democratic primary contest against James Talarico.
articles.snippet: The endorsement from the former vice president, her most significant since leaving office, comes as Ms. Crockett is facing a competitive Democratic primary contest against James Talarico.
articles.byline: By Reid J. Epstein and Shane Goldmacher

articles.title: The Reason Gen Z Isn’t Dating
articles.abstract: Clavicular is a symptom of a larger disease.
articles.snippet: Clavicular is a symptom of a larger disease.

articles.title: Ruth Bader Ginsburg, Supreme Court’s Feminist Icon, Is Dead at 87
articles.abstract: The second woman appointed to the Supreme Court, Justice Ginsburg’s pointed and powerful dissenting opinions earned her late-life rock stardom.
articles.snippet: The second woman appointed to the Supreme Court, Justice Ginsburg’s pointed and powerful dissenting opinions earned her late-life rock stardom.
